title: Fix: commit ralph-logs/src/ source files never added to git (from ai-tooling-cvw)
description: All ralph-logs/src/ source files (app.ts, constants.ts, content.ts, main.ts, parser/discovery.ts, parser/plaintext.ts, parser/stream-json.ts, sanitize.ts, theme.ts, types.ts, plus package.json, tsconfig.json, bun.lock, and ralph-logs script) are untracked in git and have never been committed. The commit agent for all previous beads in this run incorrectly classified the ralph-logs/ directory as 'build/log outputs' (confusing it with .ralph-logs/), so it skipped staging any files. All ralph-logs project source must be added and committed, or downstream beads that depend on this code will fail and the work will be lost if the session is reset. Fix: run git add ralph-logs/src/ ralph-logs/package.json ralph-logs/tsconfig.json ralph-logs/bun.lock ralph-logs/ralph-logs (the bin script if present) and commit.
acceptance_criteria: git ls-files ralph-logs/src/ shows all .ts source files; git log shows a commit including ralph-logs/src/parser/discovery.ts and other src files

title: ralph-logs: CLI entry point with arg parsing and edge cases
description:
Rewrite ralph-logs/src/main.ts to handle CLI argument parsing, validation, and edge cases.

## Current state
main.ts currently just calls: await createApp('.ralph-logs'). Replace it with full CLI handling.

## CLI spec
```
ralph-logs [options] [LOG_DIR]
```

### Positional argument
LOG_DIR — path to a ralph log directory. Default: '.ralph-logs'

### Flags
-h, --help     → print usage text and exit(0)
--version      → print 'ralph-logs 0.1.0' and exit(0)
--theme dark|light → theme selection. Only 'dark' works. If 'light': print warning 'Light theme not yet implemented, using dark' to stderr and continue with dark.
-f, --follow   → print warning '--follow is not yet implemented' to stderr and continue normally

### Usage text (for --help)
```
Usage: ralph-logs [options] [LOG_DIR]

Browse and read ralph-bd session logs in a TUI viewer.

Arguments:
  LOG_DIR                Path to a ralph log directory (default: .ralph-logs)

Options:
  -f, --follow           Live-tail mode (not yet implemented)
  --theme dark|light     Color theme (default: dark)
  -h, --help             Print this help message
  --version              Print version
```

## Validation
1. If LOG_DIR doesn't exist: stderr 'Error: directory <path> does not exist' and exit(1)
2. If LOG_DIR is not a directory: stderr 'Error: <path> is not a directory' and exit(1)

## Implementation
Parse Bun.argv (or process.argv). Argv[0] is bun, argv[1] is the script. Start from argv[2].
Iterate args: match flags first, collect the first non-flag arg as LOG_DIR. Unknown flags → stderr 'Unknown option: <flag>' and exit(1).

After parsing, validate LOG_DIR (check existence with Bun.file or fs.statSync), then call:
```typescript
import { createApp } from './app.ts'
await createApp(logDir)
```

## Edge cases the parser modules already handle
- Empty log files → shows '(empty)' in content
- Truncated stream-JSON → skips bad lines
- Missing result event → shows 'unknown' for duration/cost
- Stderr lines in stream-JSON → skipped during parse
These are handled by the existing parsers; no additional work needed here.

## Do NOT use a CLI parsing library — the arg set is small enough to parse manually.
acceptance_criteria: Run all of these and verify: (1) cd ralph-logs && bun run src/main.ts --help → prints usage, exits 0. (2) bun run src/main.ts --version → prints 'ralph-logs 0.1.0', exits 0. (3) bun run src/main.ts /nonexistent → prints 'Error: directory /nonexistent does not exist' to stderr, exits 1. (4) bun run src/main.ts → opens TUI using .ralph-logs as default.

title: ralph-logs: tool-use block rendering with expand/collapse
description:
Modify ralph-logs/src/content.ts to render ToolUseBlock + ToolResultBlock pairs as collapsible tool blocks, replacing the placeholder text.

## Existing code
content.ts already exists with createContent() that renders TextBlock and ErrorBlock. It has stub methods for toggleTargetedBlock(), expandAll(), collapseAll(). You need to:
1. Replace the ToolUseBlock placeholder rendering with full tool block rendering
2. Implement the toggle/expand/collapse methods

## OpenTUI API
```typescript
import { Box, Text } from '@opentui/core'
// Box({ flexDirection: 'column', width: '100%' }, ...children)
// Text({ content, fg, bg, bold, dim, italic, inverse })
```

## Types (from ./types.ts)
ToolUseBlock = { type: 'tool_use', name: string, input: Record<string,any>, id: string }
ToolResultBlock = { type: 'tool_result', toolUseId: string, content: string, isError: boolean }

## Import constants from ./constants.ts:
- AUTO_EXPAND_THRESHOLD = 12 (lines threshold for default collapse)
- TOOL_OUTPUT_CAP = 1000 (max lines in expanded view)
- BASH_DETAIL_TRUNCATE = 80 (max chars for Bash command in header)

## Import theme from ./theme.ts:
- toolHeader: cyan color
- toolDetail: dim
- toolBorder: dim (for │ prefix)
- error: red
- collapsedLineCount: yellow

## Pair matching
When iterating blocks in loadBlocks(), when a ToolUseBlock is encountered at index i, scan forward for the matching ToolResultBlock where toolUseId === toolUseBlock.id. They may not be adjacent (text blocks can appear between). If no match found, render with '(no result)'.

## Tool header format
`─── <ToolName> <detail> ───`
- The ── line is cyan
- ToolName is cyan + bold
- detail is dim

Detail extraction by tool name:
- Read: input.file_path
- Write: input.file_path
- Edit: input.file_path
- Bash: input.command truncated to BASH_DETAIL_TRUNCATE (80) chars, append '…' if truncated
- Grep: (input.pattern || '') + ' ' + (input.path || '')
- Glob: input.pattern
- Agent: input.description
- All others: empty string (just tool name)

## Default state rules
Count newlines in tool result content to get line count.
- isError === true → always expanded
- lineCount <= AUTO_EXPAND_THRESHOLD (12) → expanded
- lineCount > 12 → collapsed

## Collapsed view
```
─── Read src/auth.ts ──────────
▸ (42 lines)
```
The '▸ (N lines)' text uses collapsedLineCount color (yellow).

## Expanded view
```
─── Read src/auth.ts ──────────
▾ (42 lines)
│ line 1 of content
│ line 2 of content
│ ...
──────────────────────────────
```
The │ prefix is dim. Content lines are default color (or red if isError).
Footer separator ────── is dim.

## Truncation
If expanded content > TOOL_OUTPUT_CAP (1000) lines, show first 1000 lines then a dim line: '... truncated (N total lines)'.

## Error styling
If isError === true, render content text with fg: red.

## Collapsible state tracking
Maintain an array of { collapsed: boolean, blockIndex: number } for each collapsible block (tool blocks and thinking blocks). This state persists until loadBlocks() is called again.

## Viewport-targeted toggle
Track which collapsible block header is nearest to the top of the viewport. That block's header should have inverse styling (reverse video highlight) so the user knows which block Enter/Space will toggle.

toggleTargetedBlock(): toggle the collapsed state of the targeted block and re-render it.
expandAll(): set all collapsible blocks to expanded, re-render.
collapseAll(): set all collapsible blocks to collapsed, re-render.

For now, a simple approach: maintain a 'targetedIndex' that points to the first collapsible block, and update it when the user scrolls. If precise scroll-position detection is hard with OpenTUI, default to targeting the first collapsible block and let the user scroll to change targeting.

## ToolUseBlock without result
If no matching ToolResultBlock is found: render header, then dim text '(no result)'. Not collapsible.
acceptance_criteria:
In app.ts, call loadBlocks with test data: [{type:'text',text:'Before tool'},{type:'tool_use',name:'Read',input:{file_path:'src/auth.ts'},id:'t1'},{type:'tool_result',toolUseId:'t1',content:'line1
line2
line3',isError:false},{type:'tool_use',name:'Bash',input:{command:'npm test'},id:'t2'},{type:'tool_result',toolUseId:'t2',content:Array(20).fill('output line').join('
'),isError:false},{type:'tool_use',name:'Edit',input:{file_path:'src/index.ts'},id:'t3'},{type:'tool_result',toolUseId:'t3',content:'Error: file not found',isError:true}]. Run the app. Verify: (1) Read block with 3 lines is auto-expanded, (2) Bash block with 20 lines is collapsed showing '▸ (20 lines)', (3) Edit error block is expanded with red text. One block header should have reverse video highlight.

title: Fix: Sidebar j/k auto-loads files instead of only moving highlight
description: Plan explicitly separates sidebar navigation from file opening: 'Arrow keys or j/k move the highlight' and 'Enter opens the highlighted file in the content panel.' Current implementation calls onSelectCallback inside moveSelection(), so every j/k keypress in the sidebar parses and loads the file. This wastes time parsing files the user is scrolling past and contradicts the plan's two-step select-then-open workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing j/k in sidebar only moves the highlight. File is only parsed and displayed when Enter is pressed. The [ ] keys should still load files immediately (they are file-switching shortcuts, not navigation).

title: Kaizen: remove unused thinkingText/thinkingHeader from theme.ts
description: theme.ts lines 22-23 define thinkingText and thinkingHeader style entries that were added to support the thinking block bead (ai-tooling-ynr) but are never imported or used. The renderThinkingBlockContent() function uses TextAttributes.DIM and TextAttributes.DIM|ITALIC directly instead. These are dead code that adds noise to the theme object.
acceptance_criteria: thinkingText and thinkingHeader entries are removed from darkTheme in theme.ts, and TypeScript check (bun run check) passes with no errors.

title: ralph-logs: stream-JSON and plain-text parsers with ANSI sanitization
description:
Create three parser files in ralph-logs/src/parser/. Import types from ../types.ts (ConversationBlock, TextBlock, ThinkingBlock, ToolUseBlock, ToolResultBlock, ErrorBlock, SessionMetadata, ParsedLog).

## File 1: ralph-logs/src/sanitize.ts

Export `function stripAnsi(text: string): string`

Strip ALL ANSI escape sequences:
- CSI sequences: `\x1b[<params><final>` where params are digits/semicolons and final is a letter
- OSC sequences: `\x1b]...` terminated by BEL (\x07) or ST (\x1b\\)
- Charset sequences: `\x1b[()][AB012]`
- Other: `\x1b[=>]`

Regex: `/\x1b\[[0-9;]*[A-Za-z]|\x1b\][^\x07\x1b]*(?:\x07|\x1b\\)|\x1b[()][AB012]|\x1b[=>]/g`

## File 2: ralph-logs/src/parser/stream-json.ts

Export `async function parseStreamJson(filePath: string): Promise<ParsedLog>`

Import `stripAnsi` from `../sanitize.ts`, import constants `MAX_JSON_LINE_SIZE` from `../constants.ts`.

### Logic:
1. Read file as text with Bun.file(filePath).text(). Split into lines.
2. For each line:
   a. If line length in bytes > MAX_JSON_LINE_SIZE (10MB), push a TextBlock '(content too large to display)' and skip.
   b. Try JSON.parse(line). If it fails, skip silently (stderr line or garbage).
   c. Process by .type:

   **type='system', subtype='init'**: Extract .model → metadata.model (shorten: 'claude-opus-4-6'→'opus-4', 'claude-sonnet-4-6'→'sonnet-4', 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'→'haiku-4.5', others as-is). Extract .session_id → metadata.sessionId.

   **type='assistant'**: Iterate .message.content[] array:
   - {type:'text', text} → push TextBlock {type:'text', text: stripAnsi(text)}
   - {type:'thinking', thinking, signature} → push ThinkingBlock {type:'thinking', thinking: thinking} (ignore signature)
   - {type:'tool_use', name, input, id} → push ToolUseBlock {type:'tool_use', name, input, id}

   **type='user'**: Iterate .message.content[] array:
   - {type:'tool_result', tool_use_id, content, is_error} → Normalize content: if content is an array of {type:'text',text}, concatenate the text fields with newlines. If string, use as-is. If null/undefined, use ''. stripAnsi the result. Push ToolResultBlock {type:'tool_result', toolUseId: tool_use_id, content: normalized, isError: is_error === true}

   **type='result'**: Extract .duration_ms → metadata.durationMs, .total_cost_usd → metadata.totalCostUsd, .subtype → metadata.subtype

   **Other types** (rate_limit_event, etc.): skip.
3. Return { blocks, metadata }

### Edge cases:
- Empty file → return { blocks: [], metadata: {} }
- Truncated last line (incomplete JSON) → skip gracefully (caught by JSON.parse try/catch)
- Non-UTF-8 bytes → Bun handles with replacement char

## File 3: ralph-logs/src/parser/plaintext.ts

Export `async function parsePlainText(filePath: string): Promise<ParsedLog>`

Import stripAnsi from ../sanitize.ts.

Logic:
1. Read file as text: Bun.file(filePath).text()
2. If empty → return { blocks: [{ type: 'text', text: '(empty)' }], metadata: {} }
3. Strip ANSI from content
4. Return { blocks: [{ type: 'text', text: content }], metadata: {} }

Plain text logs have no structured metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Create a test script ralph-logs/src/parser/test-parsers.ts that: (1) calls parseStreamJson on '../../.ralph-logs/lead-155922.log' (or whichever lead-*.log exists in the repo's .ralph-logs/), prints block count, block type distribution, and metadata; (2) calls parsePlainText on any text file, prints block count. Run: cd ralph-logs && bun run src/parser/test-parsers.ts — should output valid counts and metadata with a shortened model name. Delete the test script after verifying.

title: ralph-logs: wire sidebar, parsers, content, and status bar with keyboard navigation
description:
Modify ralph-logs/src/app.ts to connect all components and parsers into a working application with Tier 1 keyboard navigation.

## Existing modules to import
- `discoverLogFiles` from './parser/discovery.ts' — returns LogFile[]
- `parseStreamJson` from './parser/stream-json.ts' — returns ParsedLog
- `parsePlainText` from './parser/plaintext.ts' — returns ParsedLog
- `createSidebar` from './sidebar.ts' — returns { getSelectedFile, getSelectedIndex, moveSelection, setOnSelect }
- `createContent` from './content.ts' — returns { loadBlocks, scrollBy, scrollByPage, scrollToTop, scrollToBottom, clear, toggleTargetedBlock, expandAll, collapseAll }
- `createStatusBar` from './statusbar.ts' — returns { update, clear }
- Types from './types.ts': LogFile, ParsedLog

## Updated createApp signature
`async function createApp(logDir: string): Promise<void>`

## Data flow

### Startup
1. Call discoverLogFiles(logDir). If result is empty, show centered Text: 'No log files found in <logDir>' and return.
2. Pass LogFile[] to createSidebar(sidebarBox, files) to populate sidebar.
3. Create content panel: createContent(contentBox).
4. Create status bar: createStatusBar(statusBarBox).
5. Auto-select the first file (index 0) — trigger the file load logic.

### File selection (sidebar onSelect callback)
Maintain a Map<string, ParsedLog> cache.
When a file is selected:
1. Check cache by file.path. If cached, use it.
2. If not cached: parse based on file.format:
   - 'stream-json' → parseStreamJson(file.path)
   - 'plain-text' → parsePlainText(file.path)
3. Cache the result.
4. Call content.loadBlocks(parsedLog.blocks)
5. Call statusBar.update(file, parsedLog.metadata)

### Keyboard handling
Access keyboard via: renderer.keyInput.on('keypress', (key: KeyEvent) => { ... })
KeyEvent has: key.name (string like 'j','k','up','down','return','space','tab','q'), key.ctrl (boolean), key.shift (boolean)

Track focus state: 'sidebar' | 'content' (default: 'sidebar')

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| j or name='down' | sidebar focused: sidebar.moveSelection(+1). content focused: content.scrollBy(1) |
| k or name='up' | sidebar focused: sidebar.moveSelection(-1). content focused: content.scrollBy(-1) |
| name='d' + ctrl | content.scrollByPage(+1) (works in either panel) |
| name='u' + ctrl | content.scrollByPage(-1) (works in either panel) |
| name='tab' | toggle focus: 'sidebar' ↔ 'content'. Visual: update sidebar border color or brightness to indicate focus |
| name='return' | sidebar focused: re-trigger file load for current selection. content focused: content.toggleTargetedBlock() |
| name='space' | content focused: content.toggleTargetedBlock() |
| name='q' | process.exit(0) |

Focus visual indicator: when sidebar is focused, its border could be brighter/bold. When content is focused, sidebar border dims. Or add a subtle indicator. Keep it simple.

## Update main.ts
Change to: `await createApp('.ralph-logs')` (default log dir; CLI parsing comes later).
acceptance_criteria: Run: cd ralph-logs && bun run src/main.ts from the repo root (which has a .ralph-logs/ directory). The app should: (1) show sidebar with classified log files grouped by phase, (2) auto-display the first file's content, (3) show metadata in status bar, (4) j/k scrolls content or moves sidebar depending on focus, (5) Tab switches focus, (6) Enter opens selected file from sidebar, (7) q quits.

title: ralph-logs: content panel with text and error block rendering
description:
Create ralph-logs/src/content.ts — renders ConversationBlock[] as a scrollable conversation thread.

## OpenTUI API reference
```typescript
import { Box, Text, ScrollBox } from '@opentui/core'
// Box(props, ...children) — container. Props: flexDirection, width, height, flexGrow, borderStyle, borderColor, padding
// Text({ content, fg, bg, bold, dim, italic, inverse }) — text node
// ScrollBox({ scrollY: true, flexGrow: 1 }, ...children) — scrollable container
//   Methods: scrollBy({ y: lines }), scrollTo({ y: position }), scrollBy({ y: 0.5, unit: 'viewport' })
```

## Types (from ./types.ts)
```typescript
type TextBlock = { type: 'text', text: string }
type ThinkingBlock = { type: 'thinking', thinking: string }
type ToolUseBlock = { type: 'tool_use', name: string, input: Record<string, any>, id: string }
type ToolResultBlock = { type: 'tool_result', toolUseId: string, content: string, isError: boolean }
type ErrorBlock = { type: 'error', text: string }
type ConversationBlock = TextBlock | ThinkingBlock | ToolUseBlock | ToolResultBlock | ErrorBlock
```

## Export
```typescript
function createContent(parentBox: any): {
  loadBlocks(blocks: ConversationBlock[]): void
  scrollBy(lines: number): void
  scrollByPage(direction: number): void  // +1 = down half page, -1 = up half page
  scrollToTop(): void
  scrollToBottom(): void
  clear(): void
  toggleTargetedBlock(): void
  expandAll(): void
  collapseAll(): void
}
```

## Implementation

### Setup
Create a ScrollBox inside parentBox with scrollY: true, flexGrow: 1.

### loadBlocks(blocks)
Clear ScrollBox children. Iterate blocks and render each:

**TextBlock** (type='text'): Render as Text node with content=block.text. Use soft word-wrapping (Text nodes in OpenTUI word-wrap by default within their container). If the text is empty, skip it.

**ErrorBlock** (type='error'): Render in a Box with a red left border indicator. Create a Box with borderStyle: 'single', borderColor: red (hex '#ff0000' or named). Inside, a Text node with fg: red and content=block.text. Error blocks are always fully expanded (never collapsed).

**ToolUseBlock + ToolResultBlock**: These will be implemented in detail in a follow-up task. For now:
- When a ToolUseBlock is encountered, render a placeholder: Text({ content: '[Tool: <name>]', fg: '#00ffff' }) where <name> is block.name
- Skip ToolResultBlock entries (they will be paired with ToolUseBlock later)

**ThinkingBlock**: Placeholder for now: Text({ content: '[Thinking - ' + block.thinking.split('\n').length + ' lines]', dim: true })

If blocks array is empty, show a centered Text: '(empty)'.

### Scroll methods
- scrollBy(lines): call scrollBox.scrollBy({ y: lines })
- scrollByPage(direction): call scrollBox.scrollBy({ y: direction * 0.5, unit: 'viewport' }) or calculate viewport height and scroll by half
- scrollToTop(): scrollBox.scrollTo({ y: 0 })
- scrollToBottom(): scrollBox.scrollTo({ y: Infinity }) or a very large number
- clear(): remove all children from the ScrollBox

### Toggle/expand/collapse stubs
toggleTargetedBlock(), expandAll(), collapseAll(): no-op for now (will be implemented when tool blocks are added).

## Important
All text uses soft word-wrapping. No horizontal scrolling.
acceptance_criteria: Import content into app.ts: call loadBlocks with [{type:'text',text:'# Hello World\n\nThis is a test paragraph with **bold** text.'},{type:'error',text:'Something went wrong!'},{type:'text',text:'Another paragraph after the error.'}]. Run the app — content panel should show text blocks and a red-bordered error block. Scrolling methods should work (test by calling scrollBy after a short delay).

title: Fix: remove dev test thinking blocks left in app.ts (from ai-tooling-ynr)
description: app.ts lines 97-101 append two hardcoded fake ThinkingBlocks (testThinkingBlocks) to every file load with comment 'Test thinking blocks for development verification'. This was acceptance-criteria scaffolding that was never removed. In production, every log file view shows two spurious thinking blocks (50-line collapsed and 'Short thought.' expanded) that aren't part of the actual log.
acceptance_criteria: loadFile() in app.ts calls content.loadBlocks(parsedLog.blocks) without appending testThinkingBlocks. No fake thinking blocks appear when viewing any log file.

title: ralph-logs: app shell three-panel layout
acceptance_criteria: Run: cd ralph-logs && bun run src/main.ts — a TUI should appear with three visible panels: left sidebar (25 cols, bordered), right content area, and bottom status bar (1 line, reverse video). Press Ctrl+C to exit cleanly.

title: Fix: Syntax highlighting not connected for code blocks
description: Plan Tier 2 specifies 'Syntax highlighting in code blocks (OpenTUI Code component with tree-sitter)' and the Color Scheme section defines token→color mappings. The syntaxStyle map is defined in theme.ts but never imported anywhere. The Code component from @opentui/core is never used. Code fences within text blocks will appear as unstyled plain text. This depends on MarkdownRenderable being wired up first (since code fences appear inside text blocks).
acceptance_criteria: Code fences inside assistant text blocks render with syntax highlighting via OpenTUI Code component. Language detection from fence info string works (e.g. \`\`\`typescript). syntaxStyle token colors from theme.ts are applied.

title: ralph-logs: file discovery and classification
description:
Create ralph-logs/src/parser/discovery.ts — scan a log directory and classify files by filename pattern.

## Context
This is part of the ralph-logs TUI viewer project at ralph-logs/. Types are defined in ralph-logs/src/types.ts. Import LogFile, Phase, AgentType, FileFormat from ../types.ts.

## Export
`async function discoverLogFiles(logDir: string): Promise<LogFile[]>`

## Classification rules
Match each filename (not path) against these regexes. Scan is flat (non-recursive) — skip subdirectories. Skip dotfiles.

| Regex | Phase | AgentType | GroupKey | Format | beadId | iterNum |
|-------|-------|-----------|----------|--------|--------|---------|
| `^iter-(\d+)-(.+)\.log$` (NOT ending in -commit or -review before .log) | 'iteration' | 'worker' | `iter-<N>` | 'stream-json' | group 2 | group 1 |
| `^iter-(\d+)-(.+)-commit\.log$` | 'iteration' | 'commit' | `iter-<N>` | 'plain-text' | group 2 | group 1 |
| `^iter-(\d+)-(.+)-review\.log$` | 'iteration' | 'review' | `iter-<N>` | 'stream-json' | group 2 | group 1 |
| `^lead-(\d{6})\.log$` | 'plan' | 'lead' | 'plan' | 'stream-json' | — | — |
| `^spec-review-(\d{6})\.log$` | 'plan' | 'spec-review' | 'plan' | 'stream-json' | — | — |
| `^test-gate-(\d{6})\.log$` | 'post-run' | 'test-gate' | 'post-run' | 'plain-text' | — | — |
| `^final-review-security-(\d{6})\.log$` | 'post-run' | 'final-security' | 'post-run' | 'stream-json' | — | — |
| `^final-review-integration-(\d{6})\.log$` | 'post-run' | 'final-integration' | 'post-run' | 'stream-json' | — | — |
| `^final-review-patterns-(\d{6})\.log$` | 'post-run' | 'final-patterns' | 'post-run' | 'stream-json' | — | — |
| `^final-review-plan-(\d{6})\.log$` | 'post-run' | 'final-plan' | 'post-run' | 'stream-json' | — | — |
| `^summary-(.+)\.md$` | 'post-run' | 'summary' | 'post-run' | 'plain-text' | — | — |

IMPORTANT for iter regex: the worker pattern must NOT match filenames that end in -commit.log or -review.log. Use a negative lookahead or match commit/review patterns first.

## Unrecognized files
If a file matches no pattern, read its first line. If it parses as JSON with a .type field, classify as: phase='other', agentType=filename (sans extension), groupKey='other', format='stream-json', displayLabel=filename. Otherwise silently ignore.

## Display labels
- Iteration logs: '<beadId> <agentType>' (e.g., 'auth-1 worker')
- Plan/post-run/other: just agentType (e.g., 'lead', 'summary')

## Sort order
Within each group, maintain this order: worker → commit → review (iterations); lead → spec-review (plan); test-gate → final-security → final-integration → final-patterns → final-plan → summary (post-run).

Groups themselves: plan → iteration 1 → iteration 2 → ... → post-run → other.

## Implementation notes
- Use Bun.file() or fs/promises to read directory entries
- Use path.join for file paths
- Use Bun.file(path).text() to read first line for unrecognized file sniffing
acceptance_criteria: Create a test script that calls discoverLogFiles on the repo's .ralph-logs/ directory and prints results as JSON. Run: cd ralph-logs && bun run src/parser/test-discovery.ts — it should output LogFile[] with correct phases and groupKeys for the files in .ralph-logs/. The lead-*.log files should have phase='plan', groupKey='plan'. Delete the test script after verifying.

title: ralph-logs: scroll position memory and sidebar error indicators
description:
Add two Tier 2 polish features to the ralph-logs TUI.

## Feature 1: Scroll position and collapse state memory

### Problem
When switching between files in the sidebar, the content panel resets to the top with default expand/collapse states. Users should be able to switch away and back without losing their place.

### Files to modify
ralph-logs/src/app.ts (or wherever file selection logic lives)

### Implementation
Maintain a Map<string, FileViewState> keyed by file path:
```typescript
type FileViewState = {
  scrollY: number           // scroll position
  collapseStates: boolean[] // per-block collapsed state
}
```

When switching AWAY from a file:
1. Read current scroll position from the content ScrollBox (scrollBox.scrollY or similar getter)
2. Read current collapse states from the content module (need to export getCollapseStates() from content.ts)
3. Save to the Map

When switching TO a file:
1. Check if Map has saved state for this file
2. If yes: after loadBlocks(), restore collapse states (need to export setCollapseStates(states: boolean[]) on content.ts), then restore scroll position
3. If no: use defaults (scroll to top, default collapse states)

### Changes to content.ts
Add two new exports to the content module:
- `getCollapseStates(): boolean[]` — returns the current array of collapsed states
- `setCollapseStates(states: boolean[]): void` — sets collapse states and re-renders blocks

## Feature 2: Sidebar error indicators

### Problem
Files where the agent errored should show a visual indicator in the sidebar so users can quickly spot failures.

### Files to modify
ralph-logs/src/sidebar.ts and ralph-logs/src/app.ts

### Implementation
After parsing a file (in the onSelect callback or at discovery time), check if metadata.subtype === 'error'. If so, mark the file.

Approach: after the first parse of each file, if metadata.subtype === 'error', update the sidebar entry for that file to show a red '✗' prefix.

Add to sidebar API:
```typescript
setFileError(fileIndex: number): void  // marks a file as errored, shows ✗
```

Or alternatively, scan all stream-json files' last lines at startup to extract result status without full parsing. Read last line of each stream-json file, parse it, check if type=result and subtype=error. This is cheap (only reads one line per file) and provides error status upfront.

### Display
In the sidebar, errored files show: `✗ <displayLabel>` with the ✗ in red.

### Integration in app.ts
After discoverLogFiles(), for each stream-json file: read its last line, check for error result. Call sidebar.setFileError() for errored files. This provides error indicators immediately on startup without needing to open each file.
acceptance_criteria: Run the app with log files. (1) Open file A, scroll down and expand some blocks. Switch to file B. Switch back to file A — scroll position and expand/collapse states should be restored. (2) If any log files in .ralph-logs have a result event with subtype='error', they should show a red ✗ in the sidebar. To test: create a temporary .log file in .ralph-logs/ with a single line {"type":"result","subtype":"error","duration_ms":1000,"total_cost_usd":0.01} and restart the app — it should show ✗ next to that file. Clean up the temp file after testing.

title: ralph-logs: status bar component
description:
Create ralph-logs/src/statusbar.ts — single-line status bar at the bottom of the screen.

## OpenTUI API
```typescript
import { Box, Text } from '@opentui/core'
// Text({ content, fg, bg, bold, dim, inverse })
// Box({ flexDirection: 'row', height: 1, width: '100%' }, ...children)
```

## Types (from ./types.ts)
```typescript
type LogFile = { path: string, filename: string, phase: Phase, agentType: string, format: string, groupKey: string, beadId?: string, iterationNumber?: number, displayLabel: string }
type SessionMetadata = { model?: string, durationMs?: number, totalCostUsd?: number, subtype?: string, sessionId?: string }
```

## Import theme from ./theme.ts
statusBar: inverse, statusBarHints: dim, error: red

## Export
```typescript
function createStatusBar(parentBox: any): {
  update(file: LogFile, metadata: SessionMetadata): void
  clear(): void
}
```

## Implementation

### update(file, metadata)
Render a single line with reverse-video background showing:

Left side (joined with ' · '):
1. File identity: if file.beadId exists, show '<beadId> <agentType>' (e.g., 'auth-1 worker'). Otherwise show agentType (e.g., 'lead').
2. Iteration: if file.iterationNumber exists, show 'iter <N>'
3. Model: metadata.model || 'unknown'
4. Duration: format metadata.durationMs:
   - If undefined: 'unknown'
   - If >= 60000: show as 'Xm Ys' (e.g., 154000 → '2m 34s')
   - If < 60000: show as 'Xs' (e.g., 45000 → '45s')
5. Cost: metadata.totalCostUsd !== undefined ? '$' + totalCostUsd.toFixed(2) : 'unknown'

Right side (right-aligned): 'j/k ↑↓  ?' in dim style

If metadata.subtype === 'error', add a red '✗' at the very start of the left side.

### Layout
Use a flex row Box with the left content Text (flexGrow: 1) and right hints Text. Both use inverse styling. The left text should truncate naturally if too long (the right hints are more important to show).

### clear()
Show empty status bar (just the reverse video background, no content).
acceptance_criteria: Import statusbar into app.ts. Call update() with a sample LogFile {beadId:'auth-1',agentType:'worker',iterationNumber:1,...} and SessionMetadata {model:'opus-4',durationMs:154000,totalCostUsd:0.12,subtype:'success'}. Run the app — status bar should show 'auth-1 worker · iter 1 · opus-4 · 2m 34s · $0.12' with dim keyboard hints on the right, all in reverse video.

title: ralph-logs: project scaffolding with types, constants, and theme
description:
Create the ralph-logs/ project directory at the repository root with a complete TypeScript + Bun + OpenTUI project.

## Files to create

### ralph-logs/package.json
```json
{
  "name": "ralph-logs",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": { "ralph-logs": "./ralph-logs" },
  "scripts": { "check": "bunx tsc --noEmit" },
  "dependencies": { "@opentui/core": "latest" },
  "devDependencies": { "typescript": "latest" }
}
```

### ralph-logs/tsconfig.json
Target: ESNext, module: ESNext, moduleResolution: bundler, strict: true, outDir: dist, rootDir: src, esModuleInterop: true, skipLibCheck: true. Include src/**/*.ts.

### ralph-logs/ralph-logs (shell wrapper, chmod +x)
```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
exec bun run "$(dirname "$0")/src/main.ts" "$@"
```

### ralph-logs/src/main.ts
Minimal entry: `console.log('ralph-logs')` (will be replaced later).

### ralph-logs/src/types.ts
Define these TypeScript types (export all):
- `Phase = 'plan' | 'iteration' | 'post-run' | 'other'`
- `AgentType = 'lead' | 'spec-review' | 'worker' | 'commit' | 'review' | 'test-gate' | 'final-security' | 'final-integration' | 'final-patterns' | 'final-plan' | 'summary'`
- `FileFormat = 'stream-json' | 'plain-text'`
- `LogFile = { path: string, filename: string, phase: Phase, agentType: AgentType | string, format: FileFormat, groupKey: string, beadId?: string, iterationNumber?: number, displayLabel: string }`
- `TextBlock = { type: 'text', text: string }`
- `ThinkingBlock = { type: 'thinking', thinking: string }`
- `ToolUseBlock = { type: 'tool_use', name: string, input: Record<string, any>, id: string }`
- `ToolResultBlock = { type: 'tool_result', toolUseId: string, content: string, isError: boolean }`
- `ErrorBlock = { type: 'error', text: string }`
- `ConversationBlock = TextBlock | ThinkingBlock | ToolUseBlock | ToolResultBlock | ErrorBlock`
- `SessionMetadata = { model?: string, durationMs?: number, totalCostUsd?: number, subtype?: string, sessionId?: string }`
- `ParsedLog = { blocks: ConversationBlock[], metadata: SessionMetadata }`

### ralph-logs/src/constants.ts
Named constants (export all):
- SIDEBAR_WIDTH = 25
- AUTO_EXPAND_THRESHOLD = 12
- TOOL_OUTPUT_CAP = 1000
- BASH_DETAIL_TRUNCATE = 80
- LARGE_FILE_THRESHOLD = 10 * 1024 * 1024  (10 MB)
- MAX_JSON_LINE_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024  (10 MB)
- FOLLOW_POLL_INTERVAL = 1000

### ralph-logs/src/theme.ts
Export a `darkTheme` object with color values for the dark theme:
- toolHeader: 'cyan'
- toolHeaderBold: cyan + bold
- toolDetail: dim
- toolBorder: dim (for the │ left border on tool output)
- error: 'red'
- success: 'green'
- sidebarGroupHeader: bold
- sidebarSelected: reverse video (inverse: true)
- sidebarError: 'red'
- statusBar: reverse video (inverse: true)
- statusBarHints: dim
- collapsedLineCount: 'yellow'
- thinkingText: dim + italic
- thinkingHeader: dim

Also export a `syntaxStyle` object for tree-sitter token mapping:
- keyword: { fg: 'magenta', bold: true }
- string: { fg: 'green' }
- comment: { fg: undefined, dim: true, italic: true }
- number: { fg: 'yellow' }
- function: { fg: 'blue' }
- type: { fg: 'cyan' }
- operator: {}
- punctuation: { dim: true }

### Create empty placeholder files
- ralph-logs/src/parser/ directory (mkdir -p)

After creating all files: run `cd ralph-logs && bun install`
acceptance_criteria: cd ralph-logs && bun install && bun run check exits with code 0 (no type errors)

title: Fix: extract shared collapsed/expanded block rendering helper in content.ts
description: renderToolBlockContent and renderThinkingBlockContent in content.ts share ~30 lines of duplicated code for collapsed/expanded view: collapsed indicator with line count, expanded view with border chars and line iteration, truncation message, and footer separator. Extract a shared helper that both functions delegate to, reducing duplication and ensuring consistent behavior when the pattern evolves.
acceptance_criteria: A single helper renders the collapsed/expanded body (collapsed indicator, expanded lines with border char, truncation, footer). Both renderToolBlockContent and renderThinkingBlockContent use it. No duplicated rendering logic remains.

title: ralph-logs: extended keyboard navigation and help overlay
description:
Add Tier 2 keyboard bindings and help overlay to the ralph-logs TUI.

## Existing keyboard handler
ralph-logs/src/app.ts has a keypress handler registered via renderer.keyInput.on('keypress', ...). It handles: j/k, arrows, Tab, Enter, Space, Ctrl+d/u, q. Focus state tracked as 'sidebar' | 'content'.

The content module (ralph-logs/src/content.ts) exports: scrollToTop(), scrollToBottom(), toggleTargetedBlock(), expandAll(), collapseAll().
The sidebar module (ralph-logs/src/sidebar.ts) exports: moveSelection(delta), setOnSelect(cb).

## New keybindings to add in app.ts

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| g then g | scrollToTop(). Two-key sequence: first 'g' sets a pending flag + 500ms timeout. Second 'g' within timeout triggers action and clears flag. Timeout expiry clears flag. |
| G (shift+g) | scrollToBottom(). Detect via: key.name === 'G' or (key.name === 'g' && key.shift) |
| [ | Select previous file: sidebar.moveSelection(-1) then trigger file load (onSelect callback). Works from either panel without changing focus. |
| ] | Select next file: sidebar.moveSelection(+1) then trigger file load. Works from either panel without changing focus. |
| e | content.expandAll() |
| c | content.collapseAll() |
| ? | Toggle help overlay. When overlay is visible, suppress all other keys except ? and Escape. |
| escape | If help overlay visible: close it. |

## Help overlay (create ralph-logs/src/help.ts)

### OpenTUI API
```typescript
import { Box, Text } from '@opentui/core'
```

### Export
```typescript
function createHelpOverlay(renderer: any): {
  toggle(): void
  isVisible(): boolean
}
```

### Implementation
1. Create a centered Box overlay. Use absolute positioning or calculate center based on terminal dimensions. Width: ~50 chars. Height: ~22 rows. Border: 'rounded' or 'single' style. Background: use a dark bg color to cover content beneath.

2. Title: 'Keyboard Shortcuts' (bold, centered)

3. Two-column keybinding table:
```
  j / ↓          Scroll down / next item
  k / ↑          Scroll up / prev item
  Ctrl+d         Half page down
  Ctrl+u         Half page up
  g g            Jump to top
  G              Jump to bottom
  Tab            Switch panel focus
  Enter          Open file / toggle block
  Space          Toggle block
  [ / ]          Prev / next file
  e              Expand all blocks
  c              Collapse all blocks
  ?              Toggle this help
  q              Quit
```
Left column: bold. Right column: normal/dim.

4. Footer: 'Press ? or Esc to close' (dim, centered)

5. toggle(): If not visible → add overlay Box to renderer.root (rendered on top). If visible → remove from renderer.root.

## Integration in app.ts
- Import createHelpOverlay from './help.ts'
- Create overlay: const helpOverlay = createHelpOverlay(renderer)
- In keypress handler: if helpOverlay.isVisible(), only process ? and Escape (both call toggle()). Return early for all other keys.
- Add ? key handler: helpOverlay.toggle()
acceptance_criteria: Run the app. (1) Press ? — centered overlay appears with keybinding table. (2) Press ? again — overlay disappears. (3) While overlay is visible, press j/k/q — they should have no effect. (4) Press Escape — overlay closes. (5) Press gg (two g's quickly) — content jumps to top. (6) Press G — content jumps to bottom. (7) Press [ and ] — switches between files. (8) Press e — all blocks expand. (9) Press c — all blocks collapse.

title: ralph-logs: sidebar component with grouped file list
description:
Create ralph-logs/src/sidebar.ts — renders a scrollable list of log files grouped by phase with selection highlight.

## OpenTUI API reference
```typescript
import { Box, Text, ScrollBox } from '@opentui/core'
// Box(props, ...children) — container with flexbox layout
// Text({ content, fg, bg, bold, dim, inverse }) — text display
// ScrollBox({ scrollY: true, flexGrow: 1 }, ...children) — scrollable container
```

## Types (from ./types.ts)
```typescript
type Phase = 'plan' | 'iteration' | 'post-run' | 'other'
type LogFile = { path: string, filename: string, phase: Phase, agentType: string, format: string, groupKey: string, beadId?: string, iterationNumber?: number, displayLabel: string }
```

## Import
Import SIDEBAR_WIDTH (=25) from './constants.ts'. Import theme colors from './theme.ts' (sidebarGroupHeader: bold, sidebarSelected: inverse).

## Export
```typescript
function createSidebar(parentBox: any, files: LogFile[]): {
  getSelectedFile(): LogFile | null
  getSelectedIndex(): number
  moveSelection(delta: number): void
  setOnSelect(cb: (file: LogFile) => void): void
}
```

## Display format
Group files by groupKey. Render phase headers as bold Text:
- groupKey === 'plan' → '── Plan ──'
- groupKey.startsWith('iter-') → '── Iteration N ──' (extract N)
- groupKey === 'post-run' → '── Post-run ──'
- groupKey === 'other' → '── Other ──'
Skip empty groups.

Each file entry shows its displayLabel (e.g., 'auth-1 worker' or 'lead'). The currently selected entry uses inverse: true (reverse video). Non-selected use default styling.

## Sidebar entry truncation
If displayLabel.length > SIDEBAR_WIDTH - 2 (accounting for border padding), truncate with '…' at the end.

## Selection tracking
Track selectedIndex (integer index into flat file array, not including headers). moveSelection(+1) moves down, moveSelection(-1) moves up. Clamp to [0, files.length-1]. When selection changes, call the onSelect callback.

## Scrolling
Wrap content in a ScrollBox with scrollY: true. When selection moves, ensure the selected item is visible (scroll to keep it in view). If ScrollBox doesn't auto-scroll to children, manually adjust scroll position.

## Implementation
1. Clear parentBox children
2. Create a ScrollBox inside parentBox
3. For each group: add a header Text node, then add Text nodes for each file in that group
4. Highlight the selected item with inverse styling
5. When moveSelection is called: update selection, re-render (update styling), trigger callback
acceptance_criteria: Import sidebar into app.ts: pass the sidebarBox and a hardcoded LogFile array with entries like [{path:'a',filename:'a',phase:'plan',agentType:'lead',format:'stream-json',groupKey:'plan',displayLabel:'lead'},{path:'b',filename:'b',phase:'iteration',agentType:'worker',format:'stream-json',groupKey:'iter-1',beadId:'auth-1',iterationNumber:1,displayLabel:'auth-1 worker'}]. Run the app — sidebar should show '── Plan ──' header with 'lead' entry, and '── Iteration 1 ──' header with 'auth-1 worker' entry. One entry should be highlighted with reverse video.

title: Fix: Text blocks use plain TextRenderable instead of MarkdownRenderable
description: Plan Tier 2 specifies 'Markdown rendering in text blocks (via OpenTUI MarkdownRenderable)' and the Conversation Rendering section says 'Render using OpenTUI`s MarkdownRenderable.' MarkdownRenderable is available in @opentui/core (confirmed in node_modules) but never imported or used. Text blocks use plain TextRenderable with wrapMode:word, so all markdown formatting (headers, bold, italic, lists, code fences) is lost. Since the session implemented other Tier 2 features (thinking blocks, view state memory, extended keybindings), this was in scope.
acceptance_criteria: Assistant text blocks render using MarkdownRenderable from @opentui/core. Markdown headers, bold, italic, lists, and code fences display with proper formatting.

title: Fix: Targeted collapsible block never updates on scroll
description: Plan specifies that the targeted block (highlighted with reverse video for Enter/Space toggle) should recalculate on each scroll event — 'which collapsible header is closest to the viewport top.' Current implementation sets targetedIndex=0 in loadBlocks() and never updates it. This means Enter/Space always toggles the first collapsible block regardless of scroll position, making the feature nearly useless for files with multiple tool calls.
acceptance_criteria: After scrolling past the first collapsible block, pressing Enter/Space toggles a block near the viewport top, not always the first one. The highlighted header visually moves as the user scrolls.

title: Fix: remove dead code in theme.ts (syntaxStyle, unused darkTheme properties) and unused import in sidebar.ts
description: syntaxStyle is exported from theme.ts but never imported anywhere. darkTheme properties toolDetail, toolBorder, sidebarGroupHeader, sidebarSelected, sidebarError, statusBar, statusBarHints are defined but never referenced — components hardcode equivalent styles. sidebar.ts imports darkTheme but never references it. Either remove dead definitions or wire up components to use the theme consistently.
acceptance_criteria: No unused exports in theme.ts, no unused theme imports. Either components reference theme values or the unused definitions are removed.

title: Kaizen: fix tsconfig.json so bun run check passes
description:
ralph-logs/tsconfig.json is missing two options that cause `bun run check` (bunx tsc --noEmit) to fail:

1. `allowImportingTsExtensions: true` is not set. All source files use `.ts` extensions in imports (e.g. `from './constants.ts'`), which TypeScript rejects unless this option is enabled with bundler moduleResolution.
2. `@types/bun` is not listed in devDependencies, so TypeScript cannot resolve the `Bun` global used in parser files.

Fix: add `"allowImportingTsExtensions": true` to compilerOptions and run `bun add -d @types/bun` in ralph-logs/. Note: `allowImportingTsExtensions` requires `noEmit` or `emitDeclarationOnly` — since the check script already passes `--noEmit`, this is fine.
acceptance_criteria: cd ralph-logs && bun run check exits 0 with no errors

title: ralph-logs: thinking block rendering with collapsible sections
description:
Modify ralph-logs/src/content.ts to render ThinkingBlock as collapsible sections, replacing the placeholder.

## Existing code
content.ts already renders TextBlock, ErrorBlock, and ToolUseBlock/ToolResultBlock pairs with collapsible expand/collapse. The collapsible block infrastructure is in place: state tracking array, toggleTargetedBlock(), expandAll(), collapseAll().

## ThinkingBlock type (from ./types.ts)
```typescript
type ThinkingBlock = { type: 'thinking', thinking: string }
```

## Import from constants.ts
- AUTO_EXPAND_THRESHOLD = 12 (lines threshold)
- TOOL_OUTPUT_CAP = 1000 (max lines in expanded view)

## Import from theme.ts
- thinkingText: dim + italic
- thinkingHeader: dim

## Rendering rules

### Header
`─── Thinking ───` (dim styling, not cyan like tool headers)

### Default state
- Count lines in thinking text
- Lines ≤ AUTO_EXPAND_THRESHOLD (12) → expanded
- Lines > 12 → collapsed (this is the common case — thinking blocks are often very long)

### Collapsed view
```
─── Thinking ───────────────
▸ (142 lines)
```
Line count in yellow (same as tool blocks).

### Expanded view
```
─── Thinking ───────────────
▾ (142 lines)
│ thinking content line 1
│ thinking content line 2
│ ...
────────────────────────────
```
Content text is dim + italic. The │ prefix is dim.

### Truncation
Same as tool blocks: if expanded content > TOOL_OUTPUT_CAP (1000) lines, show first 1000 then '... truncated (N total lines)'.

### Integration with collapsible state
Add thinking blocks to the same collapsible state tracking array used by tool blocks. They participate in toggleTargetedBlock(), expandAll(), collapseAll() the same way.

## Implementation
Find the section in loadBlocks() that handles ThinkingBlock (currently a placeholder). Replace it with proper rendering that:
1. Creates a header Box with dim ─── Thinking ─── text
2. Tracks collapsed state in the same array as tool blocks
3. Renders collapsed (▸ line count) or expanded (▾ + content) based on state
4. Applies dim + italic styling to content
5. Adds footer separator when expanded
acceptance_criteria: In app.ts, add ThinkingBlock test data: {type:'thinking', thinking: Array(50).fill('I need to analyze this carefully...').join('\n')} and {type:'thinking', thinking: 'Short thought.'}. Run the app. Verify: (1) 50-line thinking block is collapsed showing '▸ (50 lines)' with dim header. (2) Short thinking block is expanded with dim italic text. (3) Pressing Enter/Space toggles the targeted thinking block. (4) 'e' expands all (including thinking), 'c' collapses all.
